workflow.name: Classify Gmail leads with Groq AI and sync SLA cases to Supabase via Redis
workflow.description:
## Who’s it for

This workflow is for contractors, freelancers, local service businesses, and small teams that receive leads and customer requests through Gmail but do not have a dedicated sales or admin team.

## What it does

This template turns Gmail into an AI lead desk and follow-up tracker. It filters inbox noise, reads real customer emails, and classifies each message by urgency, intent, sentiment, department, technical details, and business value.

Each email gets a color-coded response deadline:

- RED: critical issue, respond within 1 hour
- ORANGE: urgent lead or follow-up, respond within 4 hours
- YELLOW: standard lead or request, respond within 24 hours
- GREEN: low-priority message, respond within 48 hours

The first workflow watches Gmail, retrieves and cleans the email, classifies it with AI, and publishes the structured case to Redis. The second workflow listens for Redis events and logs each case to Supabase/PostgreSQL as a lightweight CRM and follow-up dashboard.

## Requirements

- Gmail OAuth2 credentials
- Groq API credentials
- Redis credentials
- Supabase/PostgreSQL credentials
- Optional Mem0 API credentials

## How to customize

Update the blocked Gmail labels, AI prompt, response deadlines, Redis channel, Supabase schema, owner alert logic, and dashboard views to match your business process.

parameters.content:
## email sort on SLA (supabase)

### How it works

1. Triggers initiate SLA workflows from Gmail and setup routines.
2. Parses and routes incoming emails based on SLA.
3. Executes a series of SQL queries to set up necessary SLA tables and functions in the database.
4. Logs SLA cases to Supabase for record-keeping.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Ensure Redis is configured for Gmail flow trigger.
- [ ] Set up PostgreSQL database credentials for SLA setup queries.
- [ ] Ensure Supabase credentials are available for logging SLA cases.

### Customization

Nodes like 'Route Emails by SLA' may require customization based on specific SLA criteria.

## Trigger from Gmail or manual

Includes nodes that initiate workflows from Gmail flow and manual SLA setup.

## Parse and route emails

Parses email data and routes them based on SLA determinations.

## SLA database setup

Executes SQL commands to configure SLA-related tables, views, and functions in the database.

## Log SLA cases

Logs SLA cases into the Supabase database after being routed.

## Secondary workflow operations

Triggers secondary workflows or logs tasks based on email SLA routing.

## Mem0 API integration

Builds payload and posts data to Mem0 API, then waits for completion.

options.systemPromptTemplate:
You are the 'Headless Brain' of StuccoOS — an intelligent triage system for plastering contractors.

Mission: Prevent LEAD LEAKAGE and SLEEPING REVENUE loss.

Rules:
1. When in doubt, classify MORE urgent.
2. New quote requests = minimum priority 'high' + 'NEW_LEAD'.
3. Complaints about completed work = 'AFTERCARE' + minimum 'high'.
4. Unknown specs = fill 'unknown', never leave empty.
5. Words like 'urgent','ASAP','damage','leak','spoed','lekkage','schade','direct' = ALWAYS 'urgent'.
6. helen_action 'true' for everything except pure admin/spam.
7. SLEEPING_REVENUE = most valuable, always minimum 'high'.
8. Spam/auto-replies = is_noise 'true'.
9. sla_status determines entire downstream workflow. Be precise.
10. Dual-intent messages = classify as MORE urgent.
11. Works in Dutch and English.
12. Only return values from allowed options.

attributes.name: sentiment
attributes.description: The emotional tone of the incoming message. Must be exactly one of: positive | neutral | negative. A message expressing frustration, anger, or disappointment is 'negative'. A message expressing gratitude, excitement, or satisfaction is 'positive'. Everything else is 'neutral'.

attributes.name: category
attributes.description: The communication type. Must be exactly one of: quote-request | follow-up | complaint | scheduling | invoice-question | praise | general-question | spam.

attributes.name: stucco_priority
attributes.description: SLA classification for the 48h Guard system. Must be exactly one of: urgent | high | normal | low. 'urgent' = immediate action needed: damage, leaks, complaints, legal threats, words like ASAP/spoed/lekkage/schade. 'high' = new lead, quote acceptance, time-sensitive. 'normal' = general admin, planning, invoices. 'low' = praise, spam, newsletters.

attributes.name: inquiry_category
attributes.description: StuccoOS pipeline stage. Must be exactly one of: NEW_LEAD | SLEEPING_REVENUE | ADMIN_FLOW | AFTERCARE. 'NEW_LEAD' = first-time inquiry. 'SLEEPING_REVENUE' = response to existing quote or follow-up (most revenue-critical). 'ADMIN_FLOW' = operational: invoices, scheduling. 'AFTERCARE' = post-completion: warranty, complaints, reviews.

attributes.name: department
attributes.description: Where to route. Must be exactly one of: sales | planning | billing | aftercare.

attributes.name: technical_specs
attributes.description: Extract technical details. Format: 'surface:[concrete|plasterboard|masonry|wood|unknown], finish:[stucco|plaster|decorative|spachtelputz|venetian|beton-cire|unknown], sqm:[number or unknown], rooms:[number or unknown]'. If nothing found: 'surface:unknown, finish:unknown, sqm:unknown, rooms:unknown'.

attributes.name: property_type
attributes.description: Must be exactly one of: existing_build | new_build | commercial | unknown.

attributes.name: helen_action
attributes.description: Should AI draft a response? Must be exactly one of: true | false. 'true' if emotional, new lead, sleeping revenue, or negative. 'false' only for pure admin or spam.

attributes.name: sla_status
attributes.description: Escalation color. Must be exactly one of: RED | ORANGE | YELLOW | GREEN. RED = negative+urgent, complaint+urgent, damage, legal. ORANGE = negative+high, urgent+non-negative, time-critical sleeping revenue. YELLOW = high+neutral, normal+negative, any new lead. GREEN = positive/neutral + normal/low.

attributes.name: owner_alert
attributes.description: Must be exactly one of: true | false. 'true' ONLY if sla_status RED, legal action, physical damage, or project >200sqm.

attributes.name: response_deadline
attributes.description: Must be exactly one of: 1h | 4h | 24h | 48h. RED=1h, ORANGE=4h, YELLOW=24h, GREEN=48h.

attributes.name: is_noise
attributes.description: Channel Contamination filter. Must be exactly one of: true | false. 'true' for auto-replies, spam, newsletters, system notifications. 'false' for all real client communication.

parameters.content:
## gmail sort on SLA

### How it works

1. Triggers on new Gmail inbox events and filters by labels.
2. Processes emails in batches and retrieves full email content.
3. Analyzes email content for classification and filters out spam.
4. Builds and applies email labels based on content.
5. Routes emails according to Service Level Agreements (SLAs).
6. Executes secondary workflows or logs tasks based on routing outcomes.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Set up Gmail credentials with access to relevant labels.
- [ ] Configure Mem0 API credentials for posting data.
- [ ] Ensure proper Redis server settings for triggering secondary workflows.

### Customization

You can adjust the filters to include or exclude specific email labels to refine the processing logic.

## Gmail trigger and filter

Triggers on new Gmail messages and filters them by labels before batching.

## Email retrieval and parsing

Retrieves full email content and parses it for further classification.

## Email classification and filtering

Classifies email content to filter out spam and prepares for routing.

## Label and route emails

Builds email labels and routes them according to SLA.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
